briefing.headline: Rig up a Lipless Crankbait — steady fall approach for clear water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Lipless Crankbait is a strong choice for fall with clear water and stable conditions.

KVD: 'Red Eye Shad is at its best in cold water. It always makes bottom contact and finds inside weed edges. Ripping it free triggers strikes. It has a shimmy as it sinks like a stick worm — dynamite for yo-yoing inside edges. Sweep rod to the side, let it sink, repeat, moving bait 4-6 feet each swe

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: KVD: 'Red Eye Shad is at its best in cold water. It always makes bottom contact and finds inside weed edges. Ripping it free triggers strikes. It has a shimmy as it sinks like a stick worm — dynamite
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: KVD: 'The bait HAS to hit something — rocks, grass, bottom. Simply retrieving in a straight line won't yield results. If the bait isn't hitting anything, stop reeling and let it sink until it does. De
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: Guntersville's grass has evolved over the years: milfoil first, then hydrilla, now a lot of eel grass. All three still present. The grass creates great habitat and clear water (filters it). Floating eel grass cut up by wind/waves/boats makes ...

Wheeler: On Tennessee River-type lakes, fish lipless-crankbait depth (10 feet or less) near grass. On clear highland reservoirs (Cherokee, Percy Priest, Lanier), fish 25-45 feet in main river channels, deepwater drains, and sharp edges near bait c...
Robertson: During prespawn at Chickamauga, fish an offshore pattern with a lipless crankbait in grass, then go headhunting with a big swimbait for kicker bites.

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable fall conditions create the most predictable fall fishing. Bass follow shad migrations into creek arms and pockets, feeding at 3-15ft. Key on points at creek mouths and secondary flats where bait concentrates.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize and switch to brighter colors
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend
